Shuckle

#0212 Scizor #0214 Heracross
  • Contents
  • Info
  • Base stats
  • Evolution chart
  • Pokédex entries
  • Moves learned
  • Sprites
  • Locations
  • Language

Shuckle is a Bug/Rock type Pokémon introduced in Generation 2.

Shuckle

Additional artwork

Pokédex data

National № 0213
Type Bug Rock
Species Mold Pokémon
Height 0.6 m (2′00″)
Weight 20.5 kg (45.2 lbs)
Abilities 1. Sturdy
2. Gluttony
Contrary (hidden ability)
Local № 0166 (Gold/Silver/Crystal)
0168 (HeartGold/SoulSilver)
0232 (Black 2/White 2)
0014 (X/Y — Mountain Kalos)
0227 (Sword/Shield)
0170 (The Crown Tundra)

Training

EV yield 1 Defense, 1 Sp. Def
Catch rate 190 (24.8% with PokéBall, full HP)
Base Friendship 50 (normal)
Base Exp. 177
Growth Rate Medium Slow

Breeding

Egg Groups Bug
Gender 50% male, 50% female
Egg cycles 20 (4,884–5,140 steps)

Base stats

HP 20
150 244
Attack 10
22 130
Defense 230
418 614
Sp. Atk 10
22 130
Sp. Def 230
418 614
Speed 5
13 119
Total 505 Min Max

The ranges shown on the right are for a level 100 Pokémon. Maximum values are based on a beneficial nature, 252 EVs, 31 IVs; minimum values are based on a hindering nature, 0 EVs, 0 IVs.

Type defenses

The effectiveness of each type on Shuckle.

NorFirWatEleGraIceFigPoiGro
½2½
FlyPsyBugRocGhoDraDarSteFai
22

Evolution chart

  • Shuckle does not evolve.

Shuckle changes

  • In Generation 3, Shuckle does not have the Gluttony ability.
  • In Generations 2-4, Shuckle has a base experience yield of 80.
  • In Generations 2-7, Shuckle has a base Friendship value of 70.

Pokédex entries

Gold The BERRIES it stores in its vase-like shell decompose and become a gooey liquid.
Silver It stores BERRIES inside its shell. To avoid attacks, it hides beneath rocks and remains completely still.
Crystal The fluid secreted by its toes carves holes in rocks for nesting and can be mixed with BERRIES to make a drink.
Ruby
Sapphire		 SHUCKLE quietly hides itself under rocks, keeping its body concealed inside its hard shell while eating berries it has stored away. The berries mix with its body fluids to become a juice.
FireRed It stores BERRIES inside its shell. To avoid attacks, it hides beneath rocks and remains completely still.
LeafGreen The BERRIES it stores in its vaselike shell decompose and become a gooey liquid.
Emerald A SHUCKLE hides under rocks, keeping its body concealed inside its shell while eating stored berries. The berries mix with its body fluids to become a juice.
Diamond
Pearl
Platinum		 It stores berries in its shell. The berries eventually ferment to become delicious juices.
HeartGold The berries it stores in its vase-like shell decompose and become a gooey liquid.
SoulSilver It stores berries inside its shell. To avoid attacks, it hides beneath rocks and remains completely still.
Black
White		 It stores berries in its shell. The berries eventually ferment to become delicious juices.
Black 2
White 2		 The berries stored in its vaselike shell eventually become a thick, pulpy juice.
X The berries stored in its jar-like shell eventually become a thick, pulpy juice.
Y It stores berries inside its shell. To avoid attacks, it hides beneath rocks and remains completely still.
Omega Ruby
Alpha Sapphire		 Shuckle quietly hides itself under rocks, keeping its body concealed inside its hard shell while eating berries it has stored away. The berries mix with its body fluids to become a juice.
Sword It stores berries inside its shell. To avoid attacks, it hides beneath rocks and remains completely still.
Shield The berries stored in its vaselike shell eventually become a thick, pulpy juice.
Brilliant Diamond
Shining Pearl		 It stores berries in its shell. The berries mix with its body fluids to become delicious juices.

Legends: Z-A Brilliant Diamond/Shining Pearl

Moves learnt by level up

Shuckle does not learn any level up moves in Pokémon Legends: Z-A.

Moves learnt by TM

Shuckle cannot be taught any TM moves in Pokémon Legends: Z-A.

Japanese ツボツボ (Tsubotsubo)
German Pottrott
French Caratroc
Italian Shuckle

Chinese (Traditional) 壺壺

Name origin

shuck
hard shell of nuts or oysters
turtle
water-based reptile
